page: Amazon Web Services m7i.48xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the Intel Xeon 8488C processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 192 virtual CPUs mapped to 96 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.2 GHz. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance operates on the Nitro hypervisor and includes advanced CPU instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions for accelerated matrix operations.

In benchmark evaluations, the m7i.48xlarge demonstrates top-tier performance in multi-core processing and memory bandwidth. The stress-ng multi-core CPU test, PassMark CPU score, and PassMark extended instructions test all rank in the top 10% of tested servers. Memory bandwidth benchmarks, including both cached small-block and uncached large-block read operations, also place in the top 10%. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with the stress-ng single-core CPU test and PassMark memory score falling into the middle 50% tier.

This instance does not include any local NVMe SSD storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached options. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 50 Gbps. The absence of local storage is a key tradeoff for workloads requiring high-speed local scratch space, though the 50 Gbps network baseline supports efficient remote storage access.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m7i.48xlarge offers high resource density and strong multi-threaded performance per resource unit, particularly for workloads that leverage its AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets. It is suited for large-scale general-purpose workloads, multi-threaded database operations, static web serving, and CPU-based LLM inference, where its top-tier prompt processing speeds for large models can be fully utilized.
description: Amazon Web Services m7i.48xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 192 vCPUs and 96 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz. It includes 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 5 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core, but lacks local storage. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance excels in multi-core benchmarks, with stress-ng multi-core, PassMark CPU, and memory bandwidth tests ranking in the top 10% tier. Single-core performance and PassMark memory scores sit in the middle 50% tier. The processor supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX, making it highly efficient for parallel processing. With a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, this instance is suited for large-scale web serving, multi-threaded database operations, and CPU-based LLM inference.

tagline: A high-density, general-purpose virtual server featuring 192 vCPUs, DDR5 memory, and advanced matrix acceleration for demanding multi-threaded workloads.
